history: The client is a young adult dealing with the aftermath of a recent divorce, which has led to financial struggles and the challenge of co-parenting. They have a history of deteriorated friendships and a complicated relationship with their parents, contributing to their current social isolation. The client maintains a commendable academic performance despite these challenges, but experiences stress related to grades and social anxiety.
intermediate_belief: If I don't handle my financial situation perfectly, then I am a failure and everything will fall apart.
intermediate_belief_depression: If I can't manage my finances, then I will never be able to improve my life and will remain stuck.
coping_strategies: The client engages in regular physical activity, such as biking and lifting weights, to manage stress. They also use substances like smoking pot a few times a week to relax, although this can sometimes increase anxiety. Socially, they tend to withdraw and avoid interactions, sitting at the back of the class to facilitate a quick exit.
situation: Managing financial hardship following the divorce, including handling financial paperwork.
auto_thought: I can't handle this financial mess; I'm going to end up losing everything.
behavior: The client avoids dealing with financial paperwork and procrastinates on financial tasks. They may also engage in substance use to temporarily escape the stress.
